decisions.decision: New scripts/context/workflow.py module with 3 public functions
decisions.rationale: Clean separation from ContextGraph core. Module holds workflow-facing logic (suggest, validate, enrich) that composes existing graph primitives. Avoids bloating __init__.py.
Each maps 1:1 to a workflow command integration (/plan, /implement, /discuss). Single-purpose functions are easier to test and evolve independently.

decisions.decision: CLI subcommands in workflow_memory.py wrapping the Python functions
decisions.rationale: Markdown commands can only call the graph via bash. Thin CLI wrappers (graph-suggest-scope, graph-validate-scope, graph-enrich) follow the existing graph-* subcommand pattern. Commands call them via bash.

decisions.decision: Automatic with graceful degradation — no opt-in flag needed
decisions.rationale: Matches /review's _graph_impact_section() pattern. If graph is missing/stale/fails, return {enabled: false, error: ...} and let the command continue without graph data. No workflow disruption.

decisions.decision: Include fuzzy CALLS edges (0.5 confidence) with warning labels
decisions.rationale: Reduces false negatives. Each edge in results carries a confidence field. Edges with confidence <= 0.5 are labeled 'low_confidence': true so the user can make informed decisions.

decisions.decision: Build all three integrations: /plan first, then /implement, then /discuss
decisions.rationale: /plan scope suggestion has highest value (directly reduces manual guessing). /implement validation prevents scope creep. /discuss enrichment improves decision-making. Each builds on the same module.

decisions.decision: Modify Markdown commands to call new CLI subcommands at specific steps
decisions.rationale: /plan Step 2-3 calls graph-suggest-scope. /implement Step 3 calls graph-validate-scope after each task. /discuss Step 2 calls graph-enrich. Minimal invasive changes.

constraints: Python 3.10+ stdlib only — no pip dependencies
Graceful degradation — graph failures must never block workflow commands
Auto-indexing — functions call graph.index() internally, no manual step required
Must not modify existing ContextGraph class or phase modules
All SQL must be parameterized (no string interpolation)
openQuestions: Should suggest_scope() weight recently-committed files higher than old ones?
Should validate_scope() auto-expand declared scope or only warn on violations?

research.summary: Research artifact documenting current graph architecture, 10-phase pipeline, 3 integration points (review/PostCommit/CLI), and gap analysis showing /plan, /implement, /discuss lack graph integration.
